technologies.name: Undertow
technologies.description:
Undertow is a flexible performant web server written in java, providing both blocking and non-blocking API’s based on NIO.
Undertow is primarily used for:
Embedding HTTP servers in Java applications (similar to Jetty or Tomcat).
Serving web applications as a standalone server.
Handling WebSockets and HTTP/2 with low latency.
Running Java EE (Jakarta EE) applications as the default web server for WildFly (formerly JBoss).

It offers an easy-to-use API for URL fetching, data parsing, extraction, and manipulation using DOM API methods, CSS, and xpath selectors. jsoup implements the WHATWG HTML5 specification, and parses HTML to the same DOM as modern browsers.

technologies.name: Selenium for Java
technologies.description:
Selenium is an umbrella project for a range of tools and libraries that enable and support the automation of web browsers.

It provides extensions to emulate user interaction with browsers, a distribution server for scaling browser allocation, and the infrastructure for implementations of the W3C WebDriver specification that lets you write interchangeable code for all major web browsers.

technologies.name: JOSE4j
technologies.description: JOSE4J is a Java library used for handling JSON Web Tokens (JWT) and related JOSE (JSON Object Signing and Encryption) operations. It provides tools for creating, parsing, validating, and manipulating JWTs, as well as working with JWS (JSON Web Signature) and JWE (JSON Web Encryption) standards. JOSE4J supports cryptographic operations such as signing, verifying, encrypting, and decrypting data, and is optimized for performance and simplicity. It is typically used for securing web applications, APIs, and services by enabling token-based authentication and secure data transmission using industry-standard cryptographic algorithms.

technologies.name: WSO2 Carbon
technologies.description: WSO2 Carbon is the core platform on which WSO2 middleware products are built. It is based on Java OSGi technology, which allows components to be dynamically installed, started, stopped, updated, and uninstalled, and it eliminates component version conflicts. In Carbon, this capability translates into a solid core of common middleware enterprise components, including clustering, security, logging, and monitoring, plus the ability to add components for specific features needed to solve a specific enterprise scenario.

technologies.name: Bouncy Castle for Java

A lightweight cryptography API.
A provider for the Java Cryptography Extension (JCE) and the Java Cryptography Architecture (JCA).
A provider for the Java Secure Socket Extension (JSSE).
